item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's great to see some latino names, and those tíldes, and that choripán really caught me off-guard!!
The dialogues are solid, and the images help create the atmosphere. Really liked this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The dialogue and presentation are what carried this for me. It's surprising how some color changes and a different font can help the atmosphere, and the photographs did a proper job of adding to the mood. I also liked the bit about the leaves having Bible passages and the Foundation logo on the roots, I think it helps bring forth the main theme.

Overall it was a pretty enjoyable read, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent post! I'm a sucker for well written exploration logs and these were great!

P. S.
Towards the end you used clip instead of magazine. Modern handguns and rifles use magazines, while some bolt actions have internal magazines that are fed via clips. My only minor complaint. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh fuck, the foundation just tried to create mewtwo :O thorough +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, knew I forgot something. Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the accent on the e in Garlés disappears and appears at random. other than that this was really good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed the ending — hopefully it suits your tastes better now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good to see it finally made it off nutest!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a really good article. In my eyes, this was most notably thanks to the characters you've created for this. Their dialogue felt natural, and their actions didn't seem unrealistic. I wasn't too surprised by the ending, but otherwise, you did an amazing job.
+1

Edit: In light of edits made to this article, I've stricken information in my comment that no longer applies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with the above that the ending mars an otherwise great article. Dialogue seems to lose all its subtext as soon as the standoff occurs, which was really the driving component for me. If it were cleaned up I imagine the real reveal at the final note would have hit a lot harder.

Also fixed a formatting issue in the terminal layout, hope you don't mind.

Ah, I do enjoy the new ending. It still exposits a bit too much for my taste but it's a definite improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ending was indeed a bit… Not sudden, but I think it could have been meatier.

Either way, upvoted for the compelling narrative and dialogue. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Incredible exploration logs. Outdone yourself with this one.

Ending feels almost anticlimactic. Not really that big of a deal — being too transparent about the mystery of Project Beta would probably ruin its mystique. Still feels almost like a shaggy dog ending with how quickly it wraps up. Edit: Ending is significantly better now. A bit wanting in the subtlety department, dialogue-wise, but otherwise an absolute improvement.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the dialogue worked for me, and the new ending is suitably frisson-inducing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see another RounderSkip™ making it's way out of draft and onto main. Hearty +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you afraid of god? You shouldn't be.

Another entry into the collection. This piece was made for the -ES wiki's I57 canon, and carries the BHL port of the canon theme. It also fits into my personal collection of series unnamed. Thanks to everyone who put up with this draft — there's so many of you I don't remember all the names, but a special thanks to Almarduk for harassing me into finishing this, DC_Yerko for making the canon this is for and giving me his blessing to write it and the theme, and Kirby and ARD for working through the ending with me until it wasn't garbage.

+ show block
– hide block

Apologies in advance.

antucoheader.png — taken by Almarduk, licensed under CC BY SA 3.0.
fog.png — https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Starr-180312-0687-Bocconia_frutescens-habit_in_mist_fog-Kahikinui-Maui_(41240400921).jpg
cross2.png — https://www.flickr.com/photos/142911002@N05/27286154085
antucojungle.png — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Talaku_Karen_village_peacefully_located_in_the_jungle.jpg
antucobush,png — https://www.needpix.com/photo/107475/forest-bush
camp.png — http://www.freestockphotos.biz/stockphoto/17628
rounderfog.jpg — https://www.flickr.com/photos/felixdance/29963544392/
antucowater.png — https://pxhere.com/en/photo/721
lab.png — https://vinepair.com/articles/a-collection-of-century-old-photos-of-guinnesss-brewery-allow-you-to-walk-through-history/
vat.png — https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aleppo_soap_-_vat.jpg

My Author Page, Unfortunately
